questions.text: What is the central theme of Surah Al-Qalam?
questions.answer: It defends the Prophet’s character, warns against arrogance and moral corruption, and teaches accountability through powerful examples.

questions.text: Why does the Surah open by defending the Prophet from accusations?
questions.answer: To expose the falsehood of claims against him and to establish his noble character and truthfulness.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah supports mocking and insulting the Prophet as valid criticism.
questions.answer: False. It refutes accusations and honors the Prophet’s character.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Qalam teach about the Prophet’s character?
questions.answer: It affirms he has an عظیم character, showing moral excellence is part of prophethood.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach believers to judge character beyond wealth and status?
questions.answer: It criticizes those who are influential but morally corrupt, showing status does not equal righteousness.

questions.text: What does the Surah warn about obeying every habitual swearer and slanderer?
questions.answer: It warns against following people who spread gossip, harm, and moral corruption.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that loud confidence is proof of truth.
questions.answer: False. It exposes that arrogance and manipulation can be loud but false.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Qalam teach about testing people with wealth and comfort?
questions.answer: Wealth can be a test that reveals gratitude or arrogance; it does not guarantee Allah’s pleasure.

questions.text: What is the lesson from the story of the owners of the garden?
questions.answer: Greed and planning to deny the poor leads to loss; blessings can be removed overnight.

questions.text: How did the owners of the garden show greed and ظلم?
questions.answer: They intended to harvest secretly to prevent the needy from receiving their share.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that giving the poor is optional and can be avoided through planning.
questions.answer: False. It criticizes that mindset and shows it leads to punishment.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about saying “insha’Allah” and recognizing Allah’s will?
questions.answer: Planning must include humility and recognition that Allah controls outcomes.

questions.text: How did the garden story teach repentance after loss?
questions.answer: Some recognized their wrongdoing and turned to Allah, showing repentance is possible after a wake-up call.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Qalam teach about mocking religion and believers?
questions.answer: Mockery reflects arrogance and ends in regret and humiliation.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach believers to be patient under insults?
questions.answer: It reminds that prophets faced denial, and patience with Allah’s judgment is required.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that moral rules can be adjusted to serve business profit.
questions.answer: False. It condemns greed and the attempt to deny others’ rights.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Day of Judgment as a ‘day of exposure’?
questions.answer: People will face consequences, and arrogant deniers will be humbled.

questions.text: Why is it dangerous to follow influential people who spread slander?
questions.answer: They corrupt morals and community trust, and lead others away from truth.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the danger of judging by wealth and children?
questions.answer: Such judgments can be false; Allah’s measure is taqwa and character.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Qalam teach that justice includes the poor’s rights?
questions.answer: It condemns intentional deprivation and shows that denying rights invites loss.

questions.text: What is one modern-life application of the garden story for business owners?
questions.answer: Do not build wealth by exploiting others; include charity and fair wages as part of obedience.

questions.text: How can a person avoid greed’s spiritual trap according to this Surah’s lessons?
questions.answer: Give consistently, remember Allah controls provision, and avoid hoarding and ظلم.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that repentance is pointless after Allah removes a blessing.
questions.answer: False. It shows repentance and humility are still the best response.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about false confidence in plans?
questions.answer: Plans without humility can collapse; Allah can change outcomes instantly.

questions.text: How does the Surah defend the Prophet’s mental soundness and integrity?
questions.answer: It rejects accusations and emphasizes that he speaks truth and has noble character.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the danger of bargaining truth for social acceptance?
questions.answer: Compromise with falsehood corrupts religion; believers must remain firm.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that religion should be softened until opponents are satisfied.
questions.answer: False. It warns against compromising truth to please deniers.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah’s knowledge of who is guided and who is astray?
questions.answer: Allah knows best; human judgments based on image are unreliable.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Qalam teach the believer to respond to mockery?
questions.answer: With patience, integrity, and trust in Allah’s final judgment.

questions.text: How does the Surah connect slander with spiritual corruption?
questions.answer: Slander destroys community trust and indicates a heart that loves فساد.

questions.text: What is one practical habit to avoid slander and gossip?
questions.answer: Refuse to share unverified stories and change conversations toward الخير.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that the righteous and the wicked are equal in Allah’s sight.
questions.answer: False. It emphasizes different outcomes and Allah’s justice.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about arrogance that refuses to bow to truth?
questions.answer: Arrogance leads to humiliation; refusing submission is a path to loss.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Qalam teach the importance of gratitude for blessings?
questions.answer: It shows blessings can be removed when people become greedy and ungrateful.

questions.text: What is one lesson about community justice from the garden story?
questions.answer: A community thrives when the needy are not excluded; denying rights breaks social mercy.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach the believer to be consistent in righteousness?
questions.answer: Stay firm on truth despite pressure, and do not let wealth or fear corrupt morals.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests that moral corruption is acceptable if it is profitable.
questions.answer: False. It condemns corrupt character and greed.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about patience like Yunus (AS) as a warning?
questions.answer: Do not lose patience in da’wah; remain steadfast and trust Allah’s timing.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Qalam teach that Allah can save a servant after a slip?
questions.answer: It implies Allah chooses and supports sincere servants who return to Him.

questions.text: What is one ethical business principle inspired by this Surah?
questions.answer: Never build profit on denying rights; integrate charity and fairness into planning.

questions.text: How does the Surah strengthen tawhid through moral teaching?
questions.answer: It ties ethics and accountability to Allah’s judgment, not to social approval.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests Allah is unaware of private planning.
questions.answer: False. It shows Allah can overturn secret plans easily.

questions.text: How can a modern reader use this Surah for self-auditing?
questions.answer: Review speech (gossip), business ethics, charity habits, and willingness to accept truth.

questions.text: What is one concluding takeaway about the Prophet’s example in this Surah?
questions.answer: Truth is carried with noble character; moral excellence is proof of sincerity.

questions.text: How does this Surah help someone tempted by social pressure?
questions.answer: It warns against compromise and shows that Allah’s judgment matters more than people’s opinions.

questions.text: What is one final reminder about blessings from the garden story?
questions.answer: Blessings can disappear if gratitude and justice are abandoned.
